programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 180
programme.desc: Is it impossible for humans to walk in a straight line without a point of reference? Will binary explosives explode in the case of a fender bender?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 119
programme.desc: Will a motorcycle flip if a pole is thrust into the front wheel? Can someone break out of prison with a rope made of toilet paper, human hair or bedsheets?

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: No one doubts a woman’s capacity to love, but sometimes lust can turn lethal. In the ultimate fatal attraction, "Deadly Women" looks at cases of murder driven by obsessive love.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love to Death
programme.desc: Love and money are a beautiful marriage, for the lucky ones they create a life of happiness and ease. Unless the love is of money, the bride is a killer, and the groom their victim.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Global Pursuit
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a terrorist fired into rush hour traffic. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political, targeting employees of the Central Intelligence Agency.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Hunter's Game
programme.desc: In the 1970's and 80s, people went to Alaska looking for a fresh start, to reinvent themselves, or to disappear for a while. Some of those disappearances weren't by choice.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Coffee Shop Murders
programme.desc: At a coffee shop in Washington, DC, three employees were shot to death. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong or an act of cold-blooded vengeance?

programme.title: The FBI Files: Shattered Shield
programme.desc: In a city awash in narcotics the drug lords have all the power even over the police. Corruption in New Orleans grew like cancer, eating away at public safety and threatening to destroy the city. Crooked cops break the laws they were sworn to uphold.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Master Plan
programme.desc: Willow Springs, Illinois, seemed like any other quiet Midwestern community. But it was really one of the most corrupt places in the nation. This tiny village, nestled just outside of Chicago, was in the hip pocket of organized crime.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: A Tragic Accident
programme.desc: Rebecca left her baby with fiancé James and his mother Adeline, only to discover that her baby was hospitalized. Rebecca suspects that James is responsible. Adeline blames her for the baby’s death!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Results Revealed: Did She Cheat?
programme.desc: Justin is convinced that girlfriend Lainie has been cheating on him, and more importantly that he’s not the father of their child. But his own insecurities have led him to turn violent. Also, Steven doubts whether Ashley’s child is his.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Mommy Hurt Me
programme.desc: Stephanie’s drug addiction led her to neglect and abuse her kids. Scott’s fiancée stepped up as a mother and Stephanie failed multiple drug tests, he doesn’t want her near the kids, but wants her to atone for her wrongs!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: I Have Two Husbands
programme.desc: Jessica married Gian after a five month relationship. With a baby on the way, everything seems to be going great for the couple, except Jessica is married to another man, Michael!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: What Happened to My Baby?
programme.desc: Baby Garrett died of an oxycodone drug overdose while in Sharon’s care. His mother Casey left him with Sharon so she could visit her husband Kenneth in the hospital. Who’s responsible?

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: A Shocking Betrayal

programme.title: The New Detectives: Burning Evidence
programme.desc: Identifying Burned Remains: It's difficult to have a murder investigation without a body, and burning up the victim is a time-honored method of destroying physical evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Elements Of Murder
programme.desc: There are several things that can suggest murder; jealousy, anger, poison. When everyday substances reveal hidden clues that break a homicide case, those substances become the elements of murder.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Fatal Error
programme.desc: Accidental deaths, suicides, disappearances, and fires; they're an everyday part of an insurance investigator's life. But cases shouldn't be taken at face value. Forensics has become a tool for exposing insurance fraud.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Deadly Target
programme.desc: Ballistics: A corpse is found with a gunshot wound to the head; the weapon lies next to the victim. It looks like suicide, but could it be murder? It's a question best solved by ballistics experts.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Fatal Abduction
programme.desc: Anytime, anywhere, people disappear, kidnapped from their daily routines. Predators always leave clues behind, but chasing them takes time, hampering investigators' attempts to solve these fatal abductions.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The cheerleaders and members of the marching band from the University of Southern California have their lunch prepared by the remaining contestants.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The next challenge has an element of the unknown to it, as the contestants must create dishes using ingredients chosen by the roll of a die.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The contestants must use the ingredients which can be found written on a selection of pigs' collars. First though, they must catch the pigs.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Five classic sauces: hollandaise, bechamel, demi-glace, veloute and tomato, must be the basis of the contestants dishes in this challenge.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Putting a twist on the original dishes eaten by a happy couple on their wedding day fifty years ago is the challenge for the remaining contestants.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Creating an innovative gourmet sandwich in just 30 minutes is the challenge for the contestants, with the winners being flown to a wine tasting.

programme.title: Crime Investigation Australia: The Moorhouse Horrors / The Call Girl Killing
programme.desc: We investigate the story of David and Catherine Birnie, Australia's most sadistic husband and wife killing team, who tortured, raped and murdered four women in 1986.

programme.title: Crime Investigation Australia: Headless Body: The Kim Barry Murder
programme.desc: In 1981, the headless and fingerless body of 19-year-old Kim Narelle Barry was found dumped in the bush at a mountain lookout near Wollongong, south of Sydney.

programme.title: Crime Investigation Australia: The Killing Fields of Truro
programme.desc: For the 2008 launch of Crime Investigation Australia, CI presents the complete and compelling story of "The Killing Fields of Truro", one of the most infamous crimes in Australia and yet another set in Adelaide.

programme.title: Crime Investigation Australia: Kid for Ransom / Tears for Daniel
programme.desc: We examine two notorious abduction cases. The first case is the Graeme Thorne ransom kidnapping at Bondi in 1960. Thorne, aged eight, was the son of winners of the Sydney Opera House Lottery.

programme.title: Crime Investigation Australia: The Body in the Sports Bag
programme.desc: A young Sydney man was found guilty of strangling his ex-girlfriend and stuffing the teenager's body in a cricket bag.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bounty Baby
programme.desc: What's a father to do? When the cops ask Dog for help tracking down a renegade teen with a history of running hard, Baby Lyssa, eight months pregnant and counting, insists on joining the hunt. Can Dog keep his daughter safe and catch his man?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Rain Check

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Love on the Run

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Ice Man

programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big What Have We Done Episode

programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big 'Who You Gonna Call' Episode

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Declaration of Lynndependence

programme.title: Girlfriends: Friends, Colleagues, Brothers
programme.desc: Joan discovers that being the only black woman at her firm has many disadvantages and she is constantly ignored and overlooked.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Diss-regard
programme.desc: Maya checks into the hospital for her fibroid surgery and Joan has trouble managing her time between her boyfriend and her friends.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Will Hunt You Down
programme.desc: When two escaped inmates surface in Colorado Springs, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda and his Fugitive Unit to bring them to justice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Settle the Score
programme.desc: When Johnny Crawford is killed in his easy chair, police are perplexed to learn he may have been shot over a musical instrument. It soon becomes clear the young man wasn’t killed for his own sins.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My Second Case
programme.desc: A woman’s mangled body is found beside a lonely stretch of highway. When the coroner suspects foul play, Kenda is called in to solve the second homicide case of his career.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Raise the Dead
programme.desc: When a tip comes in suggesting foul play on a 71yr old man, Lt. Joe Kenda is left with only one way to reveal the truth: exhume the body from its grave.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Death Grip
programme.desc: When a speeding truck crashes into a residential building, officers find the lifeless body of Michael Faast behind the wheel. But he didn’t die in the wreck – he was shot point-blank in the temple.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Dead On Target
programme.desc: A young father-to-be is shot dead in broad daylight by a single bullet fired from afar. At the crime scene, Kenda recognizes the hallmark of a skilled sniper.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Maya, Sara, and Lilly
programme.desc: Maya, an adopted Border Collie/Spring Spaniel Mix loves to jump but is hurting her owner’s neck. Next we meet Sara, an overly-aggressive Shepherd mix and Lilly, a pushy Poodle.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Bullying Bulldog
programme.desc: Bulldog Buster is prone to biting and growling. Sadie, a 3 month old Beagle/terrier mix, is frightened of trash cans. Lacey develops anxiety at 11 years of age.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Maxwell, Brooklyn, and Tipper
programme.desc: A spoiled Welsh Sealyham Terrier has dominance issues; an excitable English Mastiff has behavioral issues, and a territorial Tibetan Terrier needs help accepting other dogs.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Anti-Social Sasha
programme.desc: Sasha, a pound pup, is perfect in the house, but outside she shows aggression towards other dogs. Angler, a Lab, is blind, and exhibits strange behavior around food.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Sophie & Daisy
programme.desc: Sophie, a Sheltie-Golden nix, tries to nip at the heels of her owner’s horse. Daisy has an annoying problem – she tries to “hump” various members of the Benda family.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Special: 100th Episode Celebration
programme.desc: Participants from more than 200 stories featured on Dog Whisperer parade down the red carpet to celebrate the 100th Episode of Dog Whisperer with Cesar Millan.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Keith Blakelock
programme.desc: In 1985, PC Keith Blakelock was murdered during a riot on a housing estate in London, triggered after a black woman died of heart failure during a house search. Nobody has been brought to justice for his murder, despite three investigations.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Bodies Of Evidence: Roger Severs
programme.desc: In November 1993, Derek and Eileen Severs disappeared without a word. When police spoke to their son, Roger, he insisted they were on holiday. However, there was no evidence of this apparent trip, and Roger was soon arrested on suspicion of murder.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Dead Man's Hollow: Nishan Bakunts
programme.desc: In 2002, an off-duty fireman discovered a body in a field charred beyond all recognition. All the police knew was that the man had died a brutal death, having been stabbed repeatedly, shot in the head, and drenched in petrol before being set alight.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Little Miss Nobody: Alan Charlton
programme.desc: An old carpet dug up by workmen in a Cardiff back garden in 1989 was the beginning of a case that would push the boundaries of forensic science. The carpet contained human bones - but whose?

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Far From Home: Kyu Soo Kim
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the harrowing case of a London landlord who sadistically tortured and murdered two of his tenants.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Resolution To Kill: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993 a serial killer stalked London. The Metropolitan Police investigated a bizarre murder in which a man was found naked on his bed with a dead cat draped over him. He’d been strangled and his flat had been wiped of evidence.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.14
programme.desc: Teenagers, romantically linked to the same man, bicker over backstabbing, an assault at a party, and car vandalism.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.2
programme.desc: Young man sues ex-lover for car repossession; demands down payment/Father sues son for loan; son claims partial forgiveness.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.73
programme.desc: Teenager denies side-swiping mailbox, sues friend for car damage. Woman's arm broken during playful punch exchange with friend.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.4
programme.desc: Pounding on and damaging a car in a fit of rage is a claim denied by a teenager accused of vandalism by his ex-girlfriend/A man sues his ex-girlfriend for payments and damages to a car he was letting her borrow.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.99
programme.desc: Lawyer with 42 years clashes with Judge as ex-friends dispute Miami plane ticket cost/Repossessed car due to red light running, unauthorized driving, and missed payments.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.157
programme.desc: Woman cuts son's unauthorized credit card, sues for car repairs charged to her. Man claims invited to Japan by girlfriend, lacking funds, asserts trip known to her.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.11
programme.desc: A daycare provider sues for slander after parents claim she was unlicensed, negligent, and had alcohol on the premises/Two boxers and a German Shepherd allegedly jump on a car and scratch the paint; ex-friends fight over damages.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.237
programme.desc: A 'doctored' contract complicates a case about a ruined wedding reception due to heat. Feuding teenagers and parents battle over liability in a wrecked car incident after a night of drinking.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.143
programme.desc: Dispute arises from dramatic 911 call of husband mauled by pit bull; family feud ensues. Woman sues daughter’s ex-friends for misusing car purchase funds.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.32
programme.desc: A fight over an engagement ring, a damaged SUV, and airline tickets further divides ex-fiancées/Former best friends fight over a computer and whether it was a Christmas gift or a loan.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.83
programme.desc: Judge Judy bets a year’s salary that a young man didn’t declare certain earnings on his tax return. He is sued by an ex-lover for loans, a hotel room, and a digital camera.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Mystery of Slide Mountain
programme.desc: A report that examines evidence and unanswered questions regarding a June 1, 1998 auto accident on Slide Mountain, near Reno, Nevada, that led to the death of Rinette Riella Bergna and left the driver, Rinette’s husband Peter with only minor injuries.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Temptation
programme.desc: In June 2007, Dewayne Barrentine met Tausha Fields and soon after they began living together. Tausha told him some unbelievable stories about herself – leading Barrentine to verify her stories online, which resulted in the uncovering of a homicide.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mr. Wonderful
programme.desc: Nicknamed "Mr. Wonderful" by one of his victims, Matt Mathews was everything to the women who lost both their hearts and bank accounts to the prolific con artist. Married at least 11 times, Mathews is believed to have scammed hundreds of people.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Murder in Spokane
programme.desc: A report that focuses on the murders in Spokane, Washington over a period of a little more than two years (1997 - 1999) of at least a dozen women, mostly prostitutes.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Texas Confidential
programme.desc: A report on the 1997 murder of Doris Angleton, the wife of a successful Houston, Texas bookie. Doris’ husband Bob who claimed that his brother Roger was Doris’ killer, was tried for the crime in state court and acquitted. But the case did not end there.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Law and Mrs. Shelton
programme.desc: A report on Catherine Mehaffey Shelton, a criminal defense attorney from Dallas. She has been practicing law in Texas for nearly three decades and making headlines for nearly as long, primarily because strange things happen to the men in her life.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Whip It Good
programme.desc: With a motocross bike and a rare stock whip up for grabs, Ton and Allen face one of their toughest competitors yet: their former employee Carolyn, who knows all their tricks, has money to burn and is out for revenge.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton's Got a Gun
programme.desc: In the affluent area of Mission Hills, CA, Allen and Ton pick up an estate unit that contains a minibike, a Depression-era slot machine and an 1871 Colt revolver.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Fall Guys
programme.desc: The pressures of running a shop are taking a toll on Ton and Allen and not even the discovery of a stunt-man's fall bag can ease the tension. When things get heated, Ton abandons the partnership and leaves town.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: You Foos You Lose
programme.desc: When their truck breaks down, Ton and Allen lay it all on the line to raise money to fix her. They score a Foosball table, Tommy Gun drums and to Ton's delight, a treasure trove of Twinkies, but will it be enough?

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Haff-Inated
programme.desc: Allen's off his game without his morning coffee, but some fun items help him re-energize. The guys find a wrestling ring, and when Allen uncovers vintage animatronics, they remind him of his days as a theme park boat captain.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Battle Bought
programme.desc: Mint 1800s French "Harmonica Pistol" and a collection of '90s remote controlled combat robots.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Unsinkable Ton
programme.desc: Unique finds lead the guys to some cool sales. They try to authenticate props from the film Titanic, Ton faces off with a rattler during a snakebite kit sale, and Allen gets into ice carving while unloading some chainsaws.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Jersey Score
programme.desc: Ton & Allen attend a silent auction in NJ, but that doesn't stop the local buyers from letting them know they're not welcome! They uncover a skeet throwing machine from the 1960's & a vintage vehicle with some real horsepower!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Miami Heat
programme.desc: All-terrain Air Commander Hovercraft, a 1970s NASA countdown clock and a one-of-a-kind 1960s Delta Relay rocket.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Shuffle
programme.desc: Ton and Allen teach a cocky new auction hunter a lesson. They spot a 1940's-era shuffleboard table and uncover an early hot rod made from the fuel tank of a WWII fighter plane!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Betsy's Last Stand
programme.desc: With their beloved bread truck on life support, Ton and Allen head to an auction to help save her. Will an industrial grinder and a classic Chevy be enough to mend Betsy, or will the guys be forced to say goodbye for good?

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: A Prayer for the Dying
programme.desc: A mother of two is found dead in the most expensive part of Colorado Springs, leaving Lt. Joe Kenda stumped—until he looks into her personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Timothy Peart’s Last Dance
programme.desc: When a teen is killed at a party, police question the guests and wonder if they’re all part of a cover-up.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Bullet With My Name On It
programme.desc: In his first year on the force, rookie patrol officer Joe Kenda quickly learns the ropes. But when he tries to singlehandedly stop a brawling mob, he finds himself facing down gunfire.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Body in the Freezer
programme.desc: A meeting between two businessmen leaves one shot in the face. Things seem cut and dry until Lt. Kenda realizes the “victim” could actually be the aggressor, shot struggling over his own gun.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Dead Weight
programme.desc: A seemingly random attack leaves Mary Moebus dead, and police flummoxed by a daunting field of suspects. But when Lt Joe Kenda uncovers a cache of new evidence, he realizes someone’s been lying.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Gut Instinct
programme.desc: When a soon-to-be grandmother doesn’t come home from work and the investigation unfolds, Lt. Joe Kenda realizes they could be dealing with a fledgling serial killer.

programme.title: Written In Blood
programme.desc: Tess Gerritsen invites Simon to the state of Oregon in the US, where an incredible real-life case of multi-generational murder influenced her book Body Double.

programme.title: Written In Blood

programme.title: Written In Blood

programme.title: Written In Blood

programme.title: Medical Detectives

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: Bob Dorotik's body was found on a mountain road near his ranch. His wife told police that Bob had gone jogging and never returned, but investigators had their doubts.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: Sheila Bryan was convicted of killing her own mother in a car fire, and sentenced to life in prison. Uncover the story of her appeal for freedom.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: When Shari Faye Smith was abducted by her murderer, her family received a letter he'd forced her to write, entitled last will and testament. Could the police catch her killer?

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: When a Massachusetts man dropped dead of an apparent heart attack, the case pitted teams of toxicologists against each other, trying to find out if it was murder.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: In 1976, a man discovered the body of a young woman in a roadside ditch in Kentucky who would be identified as 16-year-old Carol Sue Klaber, last seen riding her bike before her brutal murder.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.title: Medical Detectives

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Drive, She Said
programme.desc: Terror-stricken Mork believes the examiner assigned to give him a driving test is an incarnation of the devil, and careens along the test course in a spectacular tilted wheelie, with Mindy and Mearth in the car.

programme.desc: All that glitters is Mork's gold when the Orkan learns how to make the precious metal from polyester, buys Mindy a 60-room mansion where she can stable her new horse and hires tennis star Tracy Austin as son Mearth's tennis coach.

programme.desc: Mork is overjoyed that he and Mindy can finally let it all hang out when he meets Kalnik - an alien from Neptune who apparently is also married to an earthling and the four seem to have everything in common.

programme.title: Happy Days: The Cunningham Caper

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Two victims of alleged electronic harassment and organized gang-stalking recount their horrible ordeals. Richard investigates possible theories as to who is behind this program and why.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: The religious practice known as exorcism is examined as well as claims of demonic possession. Richard travels to a Catholic seminary in Yonkers, New York.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Is a powerfully rich cabal with a globalist agenda conspiring to destroy sovereign nations and impose authoritarian rule?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A lawyer and from the Pacific Northwest claims to have been part of a secret government time travel experiment, known as Project Pegasus.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard travels to England to investigate Britain's Roswell, also known as the Woodbridge-Bentwaters UFO incident, considered by many to be one of the best documented UFO cases on record

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several independent researchers about the efforts of international bankers to control the course of history and enslave humanity through their monopoly of the creation of money and the manipulation of the money supply.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Could vampires be real? A well-known U.S. paranormal investigator and folklorist in New York City recounts the legend and evolution of the vampire throughout history and from culture to culture.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with the pre-eminent investigators of the alleged crash of a UFO near Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the various conspiracy theories revolving around the tragic death of Diana, The People's Princess, in Paris in August 1997.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Natural History Museum, London
programme.desc: From Darwin’s original specimens to a lost workshop that inspired James Bond, discover the secrets of Britain’s Natural History Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Louvre
programme.desc: From murdered kings to stolen masterpieces, discover the secrets inside the Louvre.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: From Michelangelo’s Sistine Chapel frescoes to the fall of the Knights Templar, discover the secrets inside the Vatican Museums.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Egyptian Museum
programme.desc: From the mystery of a Pharaoh’s gold to the strange practice of animal mummification, discover the secrets inside the Egyptian Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Metropolitan Museum, New York
programme.desc: From the mystery of a king’s madness to a silver chalice some think is the Holy Grail, discover the secrets inside the Metropolitan Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Archeological Museum of Athens
programme.desc: From a dodgy archeologist who discovers a golden mask to an ancient gadget that reveals the future, discover the secrets inside the National Archaeological Museum.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Island Caving
programme.desc: Accompanied by a group of hardcore cavers from Alberta and British Columbia, George descends into spectacular caves that lead deep into the earth on Vancouver Island. The team carries inflatable kayaks to explore a dark underground river.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Crystal Cave
programme.desc: In 2000 in Chihuahua, Mexico, miners found a cave filled with giant crystals. Temperatures reach 120 degrees F and humidity nearly 100%. George leads an Explorers Club Flag Expedition into this extreme environment.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Highway to Hail
programme.desc: George and his team head into "bears' cage", looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones in Texas and Oklahoma. Their storm chasing vehicles don't look too pretty after coming through these massive storms.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Winds Of Autumn
programme.desc: This year he starts off by getting prepared in a wind tunnel, then he and his chase partner Mark Robinson head to Newfoundland for more wild weather action. Then, it's off to another encounter where the wild wind fans a raging inferno.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Java: Land of Fire
programme.desc: In the Pacific Ring of Fire island of Java, George explores new ash eruptions from Anak Krakatau, whose 1883 eruption shook the world. He travels to Merapi and descends into Kawah Ijen to explore the world's largest lake of sulfuric acid.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Eco-Disasters
programme.desc: Uzbekistan's Aral Sea, once one of the largest freshwater lakes in the world, shrunk to 1/6 its previous size; a Crimea mountain carved into a submarine base; Chernobyl nuclear site reduced to a radioactive wasteland.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane
programme.desc: Linking up with legendary storm chaser Jim Leonard, George flies to Jamaica to track Hurricane Dean. George then travels to Nova Scotia to experience the even bigger turmoil of Hurricane Noel at the iconic Maritime village of Peggy's Cove.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Fin Del Mundo
programme.desc: Although he admits to not being much of a sailor, George climbs aboard a sailing vessel to explore the extreme southern tip of South America (Fin Del Mundo aka the "End of the World").

programme.title: Angry Planet: African Hellhole
programme.desc: George explores the deadly Nyiragongo volcano in the Congo jungle but the lava lake dwelling inside it's crater may be the least of his worries as he has to negotiate through armed conflict, a family of gorillas, and severe weather.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Thunder Down Under
programme.desc: In Australia George teams up with storm chaser Jimmy Deguara to witness summer storms in the outback. While in Oz, he dives the magnificent Great Barrier Reef, and explores just how painful is the sting of the venomous Box Jellyfish.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Universe or Multiverse?

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: The Illusion of Time

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Quantum Leap

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: What Is Space?

programme.title: The New Frontier: Flight Status

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Up on the Roof
programme.desc: Today, Dog and the family are back in Colorado and are about to make one of their most exciting captures ever. When the adrenaline-fueled arrest goes down, every family member will play a critical role.

programme.desc: When the duck guys have to pull an all-nighter to finish some work, Willie decides he wants to join them. But when a dying smoke detector distracts them from their work, Jase and Willie head off to disarm it.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Burger Commander

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: G.I. Si
programme.desc: When Willie and Jase argue over an old G.I. Joe toy, it leads them into a debate over who would make a better soldier. To settle the score, Willie and Jase head out to the woods with their friends and children for a paintball match.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Jase and the Argonauts
programme.desc: When Willie gets a soft serve machine for himself, he tells the guys they can't use it until they're done working. But when Jase thinks this is unfair, he takes matters into his own hands and finds a better object to use as motivation.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fowl Playhouse
programme.desc: When Jase finds out Jep is planning to buy his kids a fancy playhouse from a catalogue, he and the guys decide to build one for Jep themselves. But the playhouse ends up looking more like a military-style duck blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Big LeCOWski
programme.desc: When Sadie spends the day at Duck Commander for a school project, the guys try to show her the ins-and-outs of how a successful company works. But when Willie questions Jase's ingenuity in creating new products they get into a debate.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Pilot - Part 1
programme.desc: In the series opener, we meet Jonathan Smith, a probationary angel who has been sent to help people on Earth. He befriends residents at a retirement home. The administrator at the home has a brother named Mark who is suspicious of Jonathan.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Pilot - Part 2
programme.desc: Jonathan helps the residents of Havencrest, when they learn that the retirement home is going to be sold. Jonathan must reveal his real identity to Mark in order to complete the assignment.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: To Touch The Moon
programme.desc: Jonathan helps a terminally ill boy and his mother. Meanwhile, Mark deals with a thief abandoned by his folks.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Return of the Masked Rider
programme.desc: Jonathan and Mark help aging movie heroes to stand up to a street gang known as Satan's Helpers. They persuade a young boxer not to throw a big fight when the gang tries to bully him.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Song of the Wild West
programme.desc: Jonathan and Mark assist a young country singer who is hoping to follow in her mother's footsteps. Next, they help a widow mourning her late husband.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Solar System UFOs: Discover the shocking truth about alien civilisations in our solar system. Check out derelict spacecrafts and explore the mysterious origins of UFOs.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Alien Bodies: Uncover the shocking truth about alien remains as experts investigate unsettling reports of extraterrestrial bodies recovered from crashed UFOs.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Signs of Abduction: From loss of time and bizarre hazy memories, to terrifying accounts of alien surgery, these are the hidden signs of alien abduction.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed

programme.title: D-Day: Secret Raids That Won The War
programme.desc: Examining the RAF 617 squadron and their history of operations throughout the war.

programme.title: Embedded 45' - Shooting War in Germany: Embedded 45 - Shooting War in Germany 1

programme.title: Embedded 45' - Shooting War in Germany: Embedded 45 - Shooting War in Germany 2

programme.desc: War Without Boundaries: The story of a deadly war that intertwines Germany, Poland, Japan, Italy and Spain as the remorseless development of weapons and bombers.

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII: The Means To Victory
programme.desc: The Means to Victory: The story of a deadly war that intertwines Germany, Poland, Japan, Italy and Spain as the remorseless development of weapons and bombers.

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise And Fall of The Nazis: The Seeds of Violence 1918-1922
programme.desc: The Seeds of Violence 1918-1922: In the wake of the First World War, Adolf Hitler identifies Benito Mussolini as his model and has one ultimate goal: to gain power in Germany.

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise And Fall of The Nazis: Deceit and Delusion 1923-1928
programme.desc: Deceit and Delusion 1923-1928: Hitler appears to be eliminated after his failed putsch in 1923. Discover how the Nazi movement used democracy's tolerance in Germany to come back.

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise And Fall of The Nazis: Democracy without Democrats 1929-1933
programme.desc: Democracy Without Democrats 1929-1933: A protest turns into a racist and anti-Jewish movement during the 1920s and the NSDAP use emotions to gain the loyalty of the people.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Brotherly Shove
programme.desc: A new week calls for new techniques as the Gloucester Captains try unconventional methods to hook their next paycheck.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Blue Grit
programme.desc: Time is running out in the North Atlantic bluefin tuna season and, this week, every boat in the Gloucester fleet is facing a different challenge.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Tuna Beta Kappa
programme.desc: The tuna fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, know that they have to rely on each other to get the job done, but sometimes trusting the competition isn't such a good idea.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Bad Blood
programme.desc: Three weeks left of bluefin tuna season, the Gloucester fleet is desperate to haul in a monster.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Sharks and Recreation
programme.desc: With only three weeks left in the North Atlantic Bluefin Tuna season, the pressure begins to take a toll on the captains.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: The Mighty Bite
programme.desc: With only two weeks left in the fishing season and the price of blue fin tuna on the rise, the Gloucester captains race to bring in record hauls.

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Wildfire
programme.desc: This week on Wardens, we're tracking down a pesky bear who has been digging through the neighbors trash. In another region, we're citing several people for fishing without a license.

programme.title: Wardens: Never Ending Day
programme.desc: The Warden's day starts early with an opportunity to capture a convicted game violator who has dodged the law for over 3 years. But just as one case closes another begins - forcing them to work late into the night.

programme.title: Wardens: Angry Like a Moose
programme.desc: Region 4 Wardens are called in to round up a 1000 pound bully. But this youngster is not leaving the safety of his home. The Wardens turn to drastic measures to secure the situation.

programme.title: Wardens: River Rescue
programme.desc: Region 1 Wardens come across a squatter on public land that leaves them speechless. And Region 5 Wardens are on high alert at the annual Yellowstone River Boat Float.

programme.title: Wardens: Failure To Tag
programme.desc: Birds, bucks and missing licenses. This week the Wardens watch a clear violation take place in Region 5. Property lines are crossed in pursuit of upland game and a license check comes up empty.

programme.title: Wardens: Warden's Instinct
programme.desc: There is often a fine line between false information and the truth. Veteran Region 5 wardens use their sixth to bring game violators to justice.

programme.title: Wardens: Fourth Dimension
programme.desc: Fourth of July in Montana – Flathead Lake is where the party is at. But with the heat tempers flare and the free flowing alcohol combines to make a dangerous situation.

programme.title: Wardens: Behind The Shed
programme.desc: When the game ranges are opened up for Antler Shed Hunting its all hands on deck for the Montana Game Wardens. Reports of Bears, Mountain Lions and lost hunters keep the wardens busy.

programme.title: Wardens: Fun in The Sun
programme.desc: When Jet skies get too close to boats things can turn dangerous. And Warden Bryan Golie questions an angler in search for the truth.

programme.title: Wardens: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Wardens often go the extra mile to investigate a report of illegal activity, help a hunter or protect landowner rights. This week Wardens go that extra mile with an unexpected outcome.

programme.title: Wardens: Thrill Kill
programme.desc: In 2012 The small community of Whitefish Montana was shocked by the useless killing of over 20 deer. Region 1 Wardens think outside the box to put a stop to this heinous crime.

programme.title: 27 Alien Encounters
programme.desc: Strange and bizarre UFO encounters abound, but recent Alien encounters by highly credible eyewitnesses are so unusual and perplexing that they challenge our understanding of physics and the Universe as we know it.

programme.title: UFO Hunters: The Italian Ufologists
programme.desc: Italian researchers explore mass UFO sightings, clipeology, and real testimonies, delving into the history and research methods of ufology.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens and the New World Order 2
programme.desc: Recent discoveries reveal that a highly advanced race of extraterrestrial beings came to Earth in our ancient past, but the shocking realization is that they are still here now, among us and may have a sinister agenda beyond our wildest imaginations.

programme.title: Alien Mind Control: The UFO Enigma
programme.desc: This fascinating documentary offers an unprecedented look at a group of individuals who claim to have had life altering UFO encounters, and discovers the unique psychic abilities they unknowingly share.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Goes To Camp/Buster Makes The Grade

programme.title: Arthur: The Good Sport/Crushed
programme.desc: When the Athlete of the Year Award doesn't go to Francine, she makes no effort to hide her hurt feelings from the recipient. Arthur can't believe his parents are hiring a babysitter--he's 8 years old, and babysitters are b-o-r-i-n-g!

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. on Ice/Spoiled Rotten!

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. Beats All/Buster the Myth Maker

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a sitting and standing game. They watch some children who are sitting and standing too. Po is riding on her scooter. She hasn’t gone very far when she finds Dipsy’s hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children hanging out their washing. Tinky Winky, Laa-Laa and Po are out walking together when a clothes line appears.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are very happy and have fun jumping all over Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies watch some children jumping. Tinky Winky makes up a dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A balloon appears in Teletubbyland; it floats in the air. The Teletubbies follow the balloon all over Teletubbyland, but they can't catch it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love playing on the slide. They stop to watch Andy Brown and some children digging in the sand for crabs.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies jump out to say "Eh-oh" before watching a girl paint a picture of her dad. A voice trumpet rises and sings the nursery rhyme Looby Loo, the Tubbies join in and dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Samira and her friends show the Teletubbies their gymnastic routines. The Teletubbies do their own gymnastics.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are feeling hungry, so they sit down to eat some Tubby Toast, but the Tubby Toaster isn't working. They watch a little girl called Becky make chocolate flake cakes for her friend Mark.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Roley is desperate to help with the apple harvest, but there’s not much a road roller can do to help. Farmer Pickles, meanwhile, asks Bob to build him a new press to make apple juice. Roley tries to help Spud collect a load of apples

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are building Sunflower Valley’s first geodesic dome. As Wendy has organised the dome and knows how it all fits together, Bob suggests that she might like to take charge of the job. Reluctantly Wendy agrees.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and the machines are in Sunflower Valley setting up Wendy’s caravan. Scoop contacts Muck to find out where Bob has got to. Muck explains that they haven’t left Bobsville yet because they’ve lost Bob’s Dad!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are taking a break from building so they can tidy-up all the left over materials from around the Valley. Farmer Pickles asks Bob if he’ll put together Scruffty’s new kennel. Bob’s got a busy morning but agrees to build the kennel later on.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles has a new family of Pigs being delivered to his farm and Bob must build a new home for them. In the meantime, Farmer Pickles places the pigs in an old Barn to keep them safe.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has been making secret early morning trips to the school where Mrs Percival has been giving him salsa lessons, as he wants to impress everybody at the town dance that night.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It is Wendy’s birthday, but she insists she doesn’t want any fuss – such as a surprise party. She heads off to start the day’s work, laying a Heritage Trail around the town, and Bob reveals to the Machines that he is not planning a party.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a day off and he takes a trip to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs for the town photography competition. Wendy, meanwhile, has been called to the school hall where a pipe has burst, threatening the competition.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are all ready to set up Mr Sabatini’s bakery opposite the windmill so it’s close to the flour. Bob promises a surprise when they get there, and who should roll out from behind a bush but Benny!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today Bob and the team have a very exciting job to do. They are building a bridge over the river to where Mr Sabatini’s new bakery will be. Bob promises Scrambler that he can be the first machine across the bridge.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Fothergill has a nasty case of dry rot, so Bob and crew will have to use some smelly chemicals to fix it. As these will affect Mr Fothergill’s allergies, he goes to his sister’s for the day. But she doesn’t get on with Hamish, his pet parrot.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: After a stormy night, Wendy has to mend a chimney pot at Mrs Potts’, while Bob repairs a drainage pipe for Farmer Pickles. A wire fence has been blown away at the farm too, but fixing the pipe takes priority.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today’s the day that the machines get to build their brand new shelter. It’s going to be built into the side of a hill and made using found materials. Lofty, who slept outside at the old yard, decides that he’d like a place in the new shelter.

programme.title: When Bob Became a Builder
programme.desc: It’s a beautiful day in Sunflower Valley and Bob and his machine team are building a new picnic area at the beach. Dizzy, Lofty and Scrambler are clearing stuff that’s been washed up on the beach, while Benny has a great time digging with Scoop and Muck.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: An observation tower is going to be added next to the Visitor’s centre to provide a scenic view of Sunflower Valley. The scaffolding is raised and Roley prepares a path to connect the tower to the Dome.

programme.title: Mega Machines: The Movie
programme.desc: Scoop, Muck and Lofty can’t wait to help Bob with his biggest build ever – building a dam and transforming an old quarry into a reservoir to provide Spring City with fresh water.

programme.title: Scottie Scheffler Wins TOUR Championship
programme.desc: Scottie Scheffler captures the FedExCup trophy with his win at the 2024 TOUR Championship. This is Scheffler's seventh PGA TOUR victory of the season and 13th career win.

programme.title: Scottie Scheffler Wins Travelers
programme.desc: Scottie Scheffler outlasted Tom Kim in a one-hole playoff to win the 2024 Travelers Championship and capture his sixth victory of the 2024 FedExCup Season.

programme.title: Stephan Jaeger Wins the 2024 Texas Children's
programme.desc: Stephan Jaeger captures his first PGA TOUR victory after winning the 2024 Texas Children's Houston Open. Jaeger shot rounds of 69-66-66-67 to finish 12-under par.

programme.title: Golf Digest
programme.desc: Join Tiger Woods for an in-depth look at how he plays the game.

programme.title: PGA TOUR - The Drop
programme.desc: Tommy Fleetwood's breakthrough win at the TOUR Championship; Keegan Bradley's big decisions.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Something behind me!
programme.desc: The team are back once again. This episode sees the intrepid team joined by two guest investigators - Kevin Ling and Michael Roberts - as they investigate a reputedly haunted venue bigger than most!

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Who's down there?
programme.desc: The team investigate a sprawling venue where apparitions, noises, and an unsavoury entity have been reported. Sara has an experience she won't forget. Dale gets a headache. And the entire team is mystified by a particular incident that keeps occurring.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Somebody walking there!
programme.desc: In this episode of the paranormal investigation series, the AYH team investigate a venue that has an intriguing history. Phil and Dale get a fright; Sara has a thought-provoking, emotional moment; and Jane becomes rather tongue-tied over it all.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Creeped out
programme.desc: The Are You Haunted team investigate a fascinating location, with a very varied history. Original AYH team member Tim rejoins the gang for the night, and everyone seems to be uneasy and on edge...but why?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: This doesn’t seem real!
programme.desc: Whilst investigating a very old and historical building, the AYH team encounter mysterious footsteps, unexplained taps and other noises seemingly on request. But at which one of the three venues on offer is this paranormal activity taking place?

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Little Boy Lost
programme.desc: In 1957, nine-year-old Peter Boland was on holiday having the time of his life on a remote farm in New Zealand when he simply vanished without a trace. Peter’s older brother Gavin Boland, a self-confessed sceptic, turns to Sensing Murder’s psychics.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Taken for a Ride
programme.desc: 54 year old Simon Buis was savagely beaten and kicked to death in an Auckland park in 1980 by persons unknown. To this day, his killers have evaded capture.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Taken Out
programme.desc: Was Donovan Reidy deliberately run down and left to die? In 1995, 23-year-old Donovan Reidy was at a party when he got into a fight. A short while later, his bloodied body was found on the side of a major New Zealand highway.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Without Warning
programme.desc: This week’s episode of Sensing Murder promises greater co-operation between psychics and police, attempting to uncover the truth about the death of 18-year-old apprentice plumber Regan O’Donoghue, who was killed in a hit and run in 2007.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters
programme.desc: Despite mysterious and unnerving warnings from locals, the Ghost Hunters face their fears and stay on mission: to help people confront terrifying paranormal encounters that have shaken them to their core.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters
programme.desc: Despite mysterious and unnerving warnings from locals, the Ghost Hunters face their fears and stay on mission: to help people confront terrifying paranormal encounters that have shaken them to their core.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Return of the Dead: Glove II
programme.desc: PRS returns to the site of one of their most famous cases, Daisytown, PA, where they had discovered a glove print of an old miner that they determined was the spirit of a Great-Grandfather.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Southern Discomfort At Sweetwater Mansion
programme.desc: PRS heads to the Deep South to investigate a haunted Antebellum Mansion in disrepair. The caretaker and volunteers at the property have many a tale to tell of paranormal activity.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Ladder
programme.desc: A large family in Shrewsbury, MA, has been living with a spirit for the last 40 years. To most of the siblings, the entity is menacing and evil, but Sandy seems to have a different connection.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Dark Practice
programme.desc: Brenda is terrified by a dark hooded figure spotted in her West Virginia home. The same entity allegedly tormented her late husband. PRS and Psychic/Medium Michelle Belanger help Brenda before she slips into the same abyss as her late husband.

programme.title: Paranormal State: First Contact
programme.desc: Washington State, a hotbed of unexplained paranormal activity. Between possible UFOs in the sky and winged creatures in the woods, Ryan and his team are forced to face their fears and deal with the question: Are we truly alone in the universe?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Spirits of the Slave Dungeon
programme.desc: Ryan books a two-night case in a real haunted jail in Charleston, SC, but PRS soon realizes the spirits themselves are being held there against their will and subjected to an unjust form of torture.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Shadow Caster
programme.desc: PRS investigates an Alabama home where clients, Georgiana and her two daughters, are so terrified, they moved out after only 18 months. They have seen shadow figures, been touched and have a recorded EVP stating, "Look at me, get out of here."

programme.title: Paranormal State: Suicide Possession
programme.desc: Joan and her boyfriend Tony are seeing apparitions in their New Jersey home. Both are concerned that the spirits are influencing their behavior: a non-drinker, Tony has turned to the bottle while Joan has thoughts of harming herself.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Knickerbocker
programme.desc: PRS checks into the Knickerbocker Hotel in Linesville, Pennsylvania where the owners are reporting strange noises and the appearance of phantom children in some of the rooms.

programme.title: NEW & EXCLUSIVE! MOST HAUNTED
programme.desc: Yvette and her team continue their investigation into this mysterious place. With something seemingly stalking the crew the night ends with a particularly vigorous spirit in a very small room.

programme.title: Most Haunted: ACCRINGTON COURTS PT1
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding leads Most Haunted to Accrington's old police station and courts, housing playful spirits and malevolent entities. Is the evil too much for the team?

programme.title: Most Haunted: ACCRINGTON COURTS PT2
programme.desc: The second part of Yvette Fielding and the Most Haunted team's investigation at the now-abandoned old police station and courts in Accrington. With the team splitting up to cover as much space as they can, something seems to be stalking them.

programme.title: Most Haunted: ACCRINGTON COURTS PT3
programme.desc: The third and final part of this terrifying and intriguing investigation into the paranormal activity at Accrington’s Old Police Station and Courts.

programme.title: Most Haunted: TOWNELEY HALL Pt1
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding is back with the first part of a brand-new episode of Most Haunted. The intrepid ghost hunter has taken her team to the historic hall, which is said to be haunted by ancestral ghosts, the apparition of a white lady and a demon dog.

programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Dorm, The Child Artist, The Weatherman, Sit-Down Comedian, Room 245.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzy baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzy's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzy changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a birthday piñata in the ranger's living room. The idea of bursting open a papier-mache statue with a bat as it says in the instructions pleases him.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Inside a mysterious cave, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a magical Game of the Goose. The events displayed in the squares that the players land on come true in real life.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is bothered by an object stuck in between the sofa cushions. It's a big fur lemming puppet glove and the Lemmings believe that the puppet is a Super Lemming who's stronger than Grizzy and start to obey it.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers the leftovers of a Halloween party in the ranger's cabin, which was entirely redecorated for the occasion.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: When the last jar of chocolate spread they were fighting over sinks to the bottom of the river, Grizzy and the Lemmings have no choice but to dive in to recover it. But to their great surprise, they discover you can't breathe in water.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a strange figurine with the power to switch the spirits of two living beings and finds himself imprisoned in the body of a caribou.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: While salmon fishing on the riverbank, Grizzy hauls in an unusual catch: an old hand mirror straight out of a fairytale; instead of showing the reflection of the person who holds it, the mirror shows an idealized vision of his surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: In a casing that fell from a circus caravan, the Lemmings find a magic stick that can enchant objects and make them come alive.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy calls upon his fishing instincts to seduce the she-bear, but only succeeds in making a fool of himself. When he sees the Lemmings riding a magnificent wild salmon like rodeo cowboys, he tries to steal theirs.

programme.title: Kipo and the Age of Wonderbeasts
programme.desc: The friends stumble upon tardigrade dream-maker Tad Mulholland who allows the gang to find their bliss. However, it comes at a price.

programme.title: Kipo and the Age of Wonderbeasts
programme.desc: Scarlemagne rallies his troops as Kipo searches for her next clue, which sends her into a trash canyon and into a hideaway for Fitness Raccoons.

programme.title: Kipo and the Age of Wonderbeasts
programme.desc: A big secret's out, and Wolf can't deal. Kipo must resist the urge to pet Mega Bunny babies before escaping from sticky Mod Frog Jamack.

programme.title: Kipo and the Age of Wonderbeasts
programme.desc: It's a high-jumping, thirsty Mega Flea vs. the Mega Dogs as Kipo closes in on the burrow, but disaster may have followed her home.

programme.title: Kipo and the Age of Wonderbeasts
programme.desc: Kipo has to go through an old friend before flying off to find Scarlemagne and save her father; it is time learn Timbercat survival skills.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: The Quiquois wonder about chocolate chickens inside chocolate eggs. Olive draws one, and a real rabbit emerges who thinks it is a hen. They plan to meet other animals with confused identities, before boarding a boat for an animal cruise.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Mixo hurts his ears grating carrots, which is annoying because it's almost time for the school music festival. Olive draws a wall for Raoul to count on while the others hide. Pierre gets chased by violins and a host of animals with their music.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Mixo continuously studies for his test even though he has perfect scores, and Pétole decides that they should do a treetop ropes course so he can stop stressing out. Olive draws a tree and ladder, and they meet Tarzan's grandson while vine-swinging.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Except for Mixo, the MiniWhats don't like eating their vegetables. Ollie draws a table, and they find themselves at Giacommo's, a restaurant that specialises in strange vegetables.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Pamela has had trouble sleeping ever since her Teddy Bear disappeared under her bed. Ollie helps the MiniWhats find it. They escape many dangers on the way to find Pamela's teddy bear Nanouk. It turns out Nanouk is happy and living a new life.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: When Petula shows up, she is hiding her cheek as she has a big, ugly spot on her face. Mustard looks at it and thinks it needs to be treated.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Ollie draws a large book for Raoul, who has to write a story for school but doesn't know how. They go into the book, where they stumble upon the Ugly Duckling.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Mustard doesn't have a grandmother to make him snacks. The others feel bad for him, so Ollie draws him a super-granny market.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: The MiniWhats are playing football, but Mixo doesn't like this sport, and the others don't want him to play anyway because he's terrible at it.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Pamela is learning to fly by jumping on the trampoline when a bird grabs her and takes her off into the air. The MiniWhats set off in a balloon to find her, and end up trapped up there in a funfair, up in the clouds.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Mustard isn't lucky, although he doesn't believe in bad-luck or good-luck charms. However, when Petula finds a four-leaf clover, he wants to keep it for himself.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: The Quiquois wonder about chocolate chickens inside chocolate eggs. Olive draws one, and a real rabbit emerges who thinks it is a hen. They plan to meet other animals with confused identities, before boarding a boat for an animal cruise.

programme.title: The MiniWhats
programme.desc: Raoul gets very disappointed when he writes such a lengthy Christmas list that there could be no way for him to get everything he asked for.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Masha and Dasha try to figure out who exactly is the best cook in all of the forest. As usual, Bear will have to be the one to solve the girls' dispute.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Bear prepares a gift for the Lady Bear and, as usual, dreams of winning his lady's heart. But unfortunately, Masha is also there, and there is no telling what her interference will lead to.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: It's another New Year for Masha and the rest of the gang. Masha has a lot of experience in organising unforgettable celebrations, so she takes matters into her own hands this time around.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Masha saves Bear from a fire and a grateful Bear makes her a gift: a toy fire truck.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Bear has a problem: his garden has been overtaken by dandelions. As Bear tries to fight against the weeds, Masha doesn't understand why he is throwing them away. After all, dandelions can be beautiful!

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Masha is the best mommy in the world, but even she cannot put Rosy to sleep. Bear offers a solution: providing a music box. However, the music box turns out to hold a surprise inside.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: A van with monkeys arrives at the Bear's house. They invite everyone to test themselves with the help of an attraction: a power meter. The Bear is confident that he will easily win the main prize.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: She-Bear is going on holiday and leaves Bear with her pet: a flycatcher. The modest flower shows an excellent appetite. Masha, of course, knows better than anyone how to treat the flycatcher.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Somebody terribly scary has climbed into the house of Penguin and made a terrible mess there. Bear rushes straight down to Antarctica to help, and, of course, Masha tags along with him.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: The gang have a snow tower and a group of friends and it sounds like a recipe for a fun game. Even Bear caved to the pressure and joined Masha, Rosie, and Penguin as they figure out who will be the princess.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Spring has dawned and the birds have arrived, but a pigeon settles in the birdhouse near Bear's house. Masha is obviously more than happy to look after him, making him her new pet.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Bear has made a new purchase: he has bought a brand new motorcycle. Now, as a cool biker, he plans on impressing She-Bear, but she does not seem to like him or his motorcycle very much.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: On a nice summer's day Bear, as usual, goes fishing and Masha follows alongside. But the fishing trip didn't quite work out: the forest dwellers made a beach at Bear's favourite spot. Masha is glad to have the opportunity to swim.

programme.title: Masha and the Bear
programme.desc: Rosie has lost her peace because of the ripe, appetising acorns growing right above her little snout. To get the desired but inaccessible delicacy, she must jump above her head or simply learn how to fly.

programme.desc: Isabel has recently lost her parents in a car accident. Feeling alone and friendless, she befriends a stray robot dog named A.R.C.H.I.E. who can talk, run at blinding speed and has super strength and x-ray vision and they become fast friends.

programme.title: Heaven Must Wait
programme.desc: An American inherits an old village church in England, where treasure is buried. Greedy Mr. Slee scents his opportunity to snatch the treasure. Diggity, a little cherub trying to earn his wings, helps uncover the treasure for the benefit of the village.

programme.title: American Black Beauty
programme.desc: After Cheryl witnesses her father’s tragic death in a riding accident, she is sent to live with her last remaining relatives, the Lane Family, on their country ranch. Cheryl is introduced to Black Beauty and the two soon form an unbreakable friendship.

programme.title: Elvira's Movie Macabre: Frankenstein's Castle of Freaks
programme.desc: When a Neanderthal man is lynched by villagers, Count Frankenstein (Rossano Brazzi) uses his years of research to bring the monster back to life. (1984)

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Misguided Missal

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Deadly Gift
programme.desc: A barmaid receives a gift, a music box from a thief, dead ten years. But someone is soon murdered and Colonel March believes the music holds the key.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Lively Ghost
programme.desc: A phony spiritualist believes she's truly summoned a real ghost. Colonel March attends her séance to discover the identity of the spirit's killer.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Death in the Dressing Room
programme.desc: Murder and blackmail culminate when a call for help during a Javenese dance is the clue that Colonel March needs to prove a faulty alibi.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The New Invisible Man

programme.title: CSI: NY: Food For Thought

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The team suspect that a woman's death might be the work of a serial killer.

programme.desc: After a hail of gunfire tears through the crime lab, Mac becomes part of a vendetta.

programme.title: CSI: NY: Exit Strategy

programme.title: CSI: NY: Indelible

programme.title: CSI: NY: Keep It Real

programme.title: CSI: Miami: Kill Clause
programme.desc: The CSIs suspect a company is killing its employees in order to profit.

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A body falls from the sky and the team discover it is from a commercial space flight.

programme.desc: The state's attorney Delko has been working with, is killed by a car bomb.

programme.title: CSI: Miami: All Fall Down
programme.desc: A serial killer is on the loose, leaving his mark on the chests of his victims.

programme.title: NEW CSI: Crime Scene Investigation

programme.title: NEW CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom and his team investigate a string of murders in Las Vegas that may be tied to an infamous mob boss from the 1970s who disappeared many years ago.

programme.title: NEW CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: When the CSIs process clues after a murder at a poultry manufacturing plant, the evidence leads them to the home of a man who collects miniature trains and other miniature paraphernalia.

programme.title: NEW CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Catherine's testimony in court is not enough to convict a killer suspected of murdering his own mother. Adam Novak, with whom Catherine had a brief romantic encounter, is the lawyer who represents the killer and secures his freedom.

programme.title: NEW CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The lead suspect for the high-profile murder of anti-drug crusading assemblyman Danilo Zamesca is Thomas Simon, the west coast's biggest drug supplier.

programme.title: Wonder
programme.desc: Born with facial differences that, up until now, have prevented him from going to a mainstream school, Auggie Pullman becomes the most unlikely of heroes when he enters the local fifth grade.

programme.title: LOL

programme.title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower

programme.title: That Hamilton Woman

programme.title: The Magic Box
programme.desc: Biography of British film pioneer William Friese-Greene, who designed and patented a working cinematic camera at the expense of his domestic life. He was largely ignored by the commercial industry until the publication of a controversial biography.

programme.title: Genevieve
programme.desc: Two couples, all car enthusiasts, challenge one another to a friendly race on the return from the annual London-to-Brighton veteran car rally. What starts out playfully becomes increasingly serious as they speed to the finish line.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Ed and Greg's sons come for a ride along and disobey a direct order from Sergeant Parker when he tells them to remain in the truck while Team One deals with an incident between three cops on an off-the-books mission to clean up the streets.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is called in when an Emotionally Disturbed Person kidnaps his neighbor's baby, thinking it's his own daughter, and takes refuge on a ferry.

programme.title: Flashpoint

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One continues the hunt for a bomber at large as team members, joined by EMS and the military, split up to deal with multiple bomb threats in a city already plunged into chaos.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When a disturbed man takes a woman hostage outside an office building, the SRU is quick to respond to save the innocent hostage and bystanders caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: Flashpoint

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Sex & Malice

programme.title: Andromeda: A Symmetry of Imperfection

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: The Abyss kills Beka's friend, and the crew must figure out what information they killed for.

programme.title: Andromeda
programme.desc: Andromeda rescues a ship returning from peace talks with the Magog.

programme.title: Andromeda

programme.title: Andromeda

programme.title: Scorpion: Who Let the Dog Out ('Cause Now It's Stuck in a Cistern)
programme.desc: As Team Scorpion anxiously awaits the verdict of Cabe's trial which could send him to prison, they focus their nervous energy on trying to save a dog stuck in a cistern.

programme.desc: Michael is assigned to a State Department mission that involves flushing out a political assassin at a Third World peace conference.

programme.title: Knight Rider

programme.title: Psych

programme.title: Psych

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 5

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 47

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 23

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 89

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love to Death

programme.title: Deadly Women: Teen Killers

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Women who kill husbands, lovers, rivals and parents will shock society. But a very special horror is reserved for women who will kill their own.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The heaviest judgments of convicted killers are reserved for those who committed premeditated murder. These women plot and plan. They know the consequences of their actions, but still they go forward to kill.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Nurse Beverley Allitt overdosed multiple infants, Stella Nickell poisoned medicine capsules in stores and Kathleen Follbigg killed four of her own children.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: We want to believe that all women would give their lives to protect their children. But there are horrifying cases when a mother will turn against her natural instinct and kill her own flesh and blood.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Master Plan
programme.desc: Willow Springs, Illinois, seemed like any other quiet Midwestern community. But it was really one of the most corrupt places in the nation. This tiny village, nestled just outside of Chicago, was in the hip pocket of organized crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Obsession

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: A Shocking Betrayal

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Steve Rescues a Runaway

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: She Said She'd Drown the Baby

programme.desc: Will Nadia and Woody's lie detector test results prove that they were negligent and deserved to lose custody of their children? Then, can Steve set the record straight about Josette's daughter?

programme.title: The New Detectives: Face Of Tragedy

programme.title: The New Detectives: Double Helix

programme.title: The New Detectives: Coroner Investigator

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The contestants go to a supermarket with the challenge to spend no more than 10 dollars in 10 minutes for their ingredients.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The four chefs must teach bachelors with no cooking experience how to prepare monkfish, the winner and a chosen guest get to ride on a Goodyear blimp.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Dead On Target

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Timothy Peart’s Last Dance

programme.desc: The murder of a young father stuns his close-knit community, but the investigation reveals a neighborly dispute turned deadly.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Resolution To Kill: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993 a serial killer stalked London. The Metropolitan Police investigated a bizarre murder in which a man was found naked on his bed with a dead cat draped over him. He’d been strangled and his flat had been wiped of evidence.

programme.desc: Young woman sues foster family over deleted files/Ex-lover sued for loan, truck damage, and impound fees from prison romance.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.126

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.68

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.222
programme.desc: A man sues his child's mother for an unpaid cable bill and missing belongings, admitting he owes her back child support. A father and his adult son fight over $60,000 in back child support and $5,000 in allegedly misappropriated emergency money.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.116
programme.desc: Claiming a female client was smoking marijuana, a personal trainer is sued by her father for allegedly skipping out on her sessions/A man allegedly locks his roommate out instead of properly evicting him after a dispute over bills.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.79
programme.desc: A teenager puts his father in a choke-hold to demonstrate how he allegedly assaulted another boy at school who passed out cold and is suing for medical expenses/Two young women accuse an ex-friend of breaking their door down.

programme.title: 48 Hours: A Raging Son
programme.desc: A cell phone captures the murder of a Weight Watchers executive by a Wall Street lawyer.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Who Killed the Prosecutor?

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Sugar Land Conspiracy

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Betsy's Last Stand

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Allen's Big Crush
